parsed_review.summary: CP00-923 is a descriptor-only RP30 People-HR embedded-HRX bridge pack over RP30.P03.M07.S03-RP30.P03.M09.S02 (10 implementation units: 3 EmployeeApi tail rows M07.S03-S05, 5 LeaveApi re-entry rows M08.S01-S05, 2 CandidateApi re-entry rows M09.S01-S02). Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the descriptor-only boundary is supported and no blocking defect exists. registry.js HRX_CP923_PACK_BINDING (lines 525-540) and HRX_CP923_REQUIREMENTS (1909-1948) match the baseline/manifest exactly (risk A, unit_count 10, first RP30.P03.M07.S03, last RP30.P03.M09.S02, upstream CP00-922, next CP00-924, next_subphase RP30.P03.M09.S03, deliverable {implementation:10}, micro_phase {M07:3,M08:5,M09:2}, micro_title {EmployeeApi:3,LeaveApi:5,CandidateApi:2}). createHrxDomainRows (registry.js:1999) builds every row with descriptor_only:true and runtime_execution/payroll_runtime/hr_ai_final_judgment/permission-audit-product writes/runtime_receipt/real employee-candidate-payroll data/credential-secret/separate_product and all employee_api/leave_api/candidate_api payload+runtime+record-write fields hardcoded false; entity_name and sensitive_hr_guard_required/h30_review_only/c30_read_only/human_final_approval_required are set correctly. A targeted leak grep for any of those fields set to true in registry.js and contracts/hrx-people-contract.json returned no matches. createHrxCp923EmployeeLeaveCandidateApiBridgeDescriptor (registry.js:5821) sets source_descriptor=CP922, authority_boundary (claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-924), domain_entities EmployeeApi/LeaveApi/CandidateApi all-false, baseline deferrals true, hr_ai_final_judgment_allowed:false, real-data allowed:false, hermes_packet.emits_runtime_receipt:false, claude_packet.read_only:true/promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, handoff to CP00-924/RP30.P03.M09.S03. validators.js (coverage 4562, descriptor 4601, contract 4720) enforce all those boundaries plus capability/safety-gate/no-leak-guard coverage and require latest_pack=CP00-923; model.test.js (1432-1508) asserts the CP00-923 distribution and closed boundaries and reads the real contract JSON with validation.errors deep-equal []. The generated contracts/hrx-people-contract.json carries current_pack/latest_pack/projection=CP00-923 (HrxCp923EmployeeLeaveCandidateApiBridgeDescriptor) with a 3-section/10-row case set and all boundary flags false. scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs chains the full CP897->CP923 binding/coverage/descriptor/contract/capability/gate ladder; all referenced npm scripts exist in package.json; index.js wildcard re-exports the CP923 symbols. closeout-pack-plan.json was regenerated to live state with live_next_unit_id RP30.P03.M07.S03 and actual_first_planned_pack_id CP00-923, so CP00-923 is the legitimate queue head; changed-file-scope correctly isolates unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook/*, docx, LDIP review attempt, new-session-handoff) as preserved and not part of this pack, with no active untracked implementation files omitted. Authority boundary is correct: Claude is a read-only independent reviewer (not final approver), the Hermes packet is deterministic review-packet/receipt metadata only (emits_runtime_receipt:false) and not embedded in product runtime, and the readiness auth preflight passed (1434-byte valid JSON, is_error:false) with no prior invalid review attempts recorded -- no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced/tool-shaped evidence, or missing fields. The pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim (manifest production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false; construction-inspection production_ready:false; claude-review-result production_ready:null), so final human approval for enterprise trust correctly remains outside this receipt and does not block this CP closeout goal. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Two non-blocking P3s, identical in nature to prior pack CP00-922: (1) pack-level review artifacts are in the sanctioned pre-promotion state and must be normalized from this single review before production_ready flips; (2) command-evidence.json exit codes are agent-reported and were verified here only by structural inspection, not re-executed under the read-only constraint. Provenance: implementation and structure were verified directly by source inspection; command pass/fail and the full closeout validation ladder (closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack/test/validate/spec/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane/git diff --check) were not re-run in this read-only session. Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block pack closeout or this CP closeout goal.

findings.title: Post-review adjudication, construction inspection, and review receipt normalization still pending
findings.details: At review time the pack-level review artifacts are in their expected pre-promotion state, not a final state: adjudication.md reads 'Overall verdict: PENDING' / 'Production ready after adjudication: no'; construction-inspection.json has production_ready:false with runtime_ready/status/boundary all null; claude-review-result.json status is 'pending_review' with null production_ready/review/findings/dispositions; and manifest.json pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending_review' with overall_verdict 'PENDING', empty valid_review_receipts, empty invalid_review_attempts, and null blocks_pack_closeout/blocks_goal_closeout. No review-receipt.json or raw-output.json exists under artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-923/ yet. This is the sanctioned pre-review state (mirrors CP00-922 finding CP922-P3-01) and not a code or boundary defect, but the pack cannot be promoted to descriptor-only production_ready until closeout automation records exactly one valid hardened read-only review receipt from this review and normalizes the adjudication, construction-inspection, claude-review-result, and manifest review block accordingly.
findings.recommendation: Have closeout automation consume this review receipt, then normalize adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, claude-review-result.json, and manifest.pack_level_claude_review from this single review (exactly one valid receipt, no blocking P0/P1/P2 findings) and re-run the full deterministic validation ladder before flipping production_ready to true.

findings.title: Closeout command exit codes are agent-reported, not re-executed under read-only review
findings.details: command-evidence.json records six commands (node --check on registry/validators/validate script; node --test packages/hrx/test/*.test.js; npm run rp30:hrx:validate, rp30:validate, hrx:requirements:validate, hrx:weighted:validate) all at exit_code 0. Under the read-only review constraint (Read/Grep/Glob only, no Bash) these exit codes were not independently re-executed. Provenance distinction: the implementation and structure were verified directly -- the referenced npm scripts exist in package.json, scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs soundly chains the CP897->CP923 binding/coverage/descriptor/contract ladder, index.js wildcard re-exports the CP923 symbols the script imports, and packages/hrx/test/model.test.js asserts the CP00-923 boundaries with validation.errors deep-equal [] -- but the command pass/fail itself is reported, not re-run in this session. The prompt's full closeout ladder (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) likewise cannot be executed here.
findings.recommendation: Closeout automation with execution rights must run the full validation ladder and capture exit codes before promotion; treat the recorded exit codes as evidence to be re-confirmed by the deterministic harness rather than as reviewer-verified results.
